commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Nextcloud Talk webhook decoder so strict schema parse misses are acknowledged as ignored instead of returning 400.

PR surface: Source +2. Total +2 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source-level inspection shows current main sends signed schema misses through the strict Note schema and returns 400 before any ignore branch can run; I did not run a live Nextcloud instance in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed for the classifier defect and missing real behavior proof; this should not enter repair automation while the external proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): silently ignore non-message Talk events instead of returning 400
target.body:
## Summary

Nextcloud Talk sends various event types (calls, system notifications, file shares) that may not have `object.type === "Note"` or `actor.type === "Person"`. The webhook handler's Zod schema was too strict — rejecting any payload that didn't match the exact message shape with a 400 "Invalid payload format" error.

This fix silently ignores non-matching payloads instead of returning 400, matching how other channel handlers gracefully handle unknown events.

Fixes #81566

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No` — non-message events were already ignored after the 400 was returned; now they are silently acknowledged)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)
